programmes.show_description: It's April and it's springtime as our first-time farmers are learning new crafts while another young farmer is fine-tuning his mechanic skills.

Kevin Bishop: Jimmy and Shivi are joined by actor and comedian Kevin Bishop for cooking, houseplant tips, bushcraft projects and snappy wildlife encounters.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
It's the halfway point on The Floor - Rob Brydon hosts as 49 contestants duel for dominance and a chance to win 50,000 pounds.
Children's TV presenter Nigel Clarke urges commuters to take extra care after revealing his daughter was left with a black eye when thieves snatched her phone.
Magazine show with news and topical items, celebrity guests, expert advice, real-life stories, competitions and a mix of cookery, health, beauty, lifestyle and more.
The latest headlines from around the world at lunchtime with reports and analysis from ITV's Correspondents, all of which are followed by a national weather forecast.

programmes.show_name: New: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programmes.show_description: Tom Allen celebrates funny cock-ups featuring our favourite TV shows and stars including Kylie Minogue, Michael McIntyre, King Charles III, Sue Perkins and many more.
Isaac is upset his dad will die of cancer. Cain is preoccupied in his search for farmland. Pollard tests Kerry's loyalty.
Daniel seeks answers from Megan about the future of their relationship. Meanwhile, Theo hatches a new plan, announcing that he's been offered a job in Belfast.
The first episode of the critically-lauded US drama. A chance encounter during a break in Long Island leads a man with a wife and four children to begin an affair with a waitress.

programmes.show_name: New: Devil in Disguise: John Wayne Gacy
programmes.show_description: David: US drama based on real events. Kunkle and Sam are knee-deep in Gacy's trial as the disturbing tale of how he befriended and groomed David Cram is revealed.

programmes.show_name: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programmes.show_description: Joining Alan at Manor Farm are actor James Murray, Lynda La Plante, florist Jonathan Moseley with innovative ideas for Mothering Sunday and a rare UK sheep breed.
James welcomes singer Alfie Boe and Loose Women's Penny Lancaster to his Hampshire home while also tasting delicious recipes from chefs Paul Rankin and Shivi Ramoutar.

programmes.show_name: EFL Live: Ipswich Town v Millwall
programmes.show_description: A key clash in the Championship as Ipswich Town take on Millwall live from Portman Road, with all eyes on promotion to the Premier League.
